verses.3: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the seas,
verses.4: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me.
verses.5: Does the wild donkey bray when he has grass?
verses.6: Can that which has no flavor be eaten without salt?
verses.7: My soul refuses to touch them.
verses.8: “Oh that I might have my request,
verses.9: even that it would please God to crush me;
verses.10: Let it still be my consolation,
verses.11: What is my strength, that I should wait?
verses.12: Is my strength the strength of stones?
verses.13: Isn’t it that I have no help in me,
verses.14: “To him who is ready to faint, kindness should be shown from his friend;
verses.15: My brothers have dealt deceitfully as a brook,
verses.16: which are black by reason of the ice,
verses.17: In the dry season, they vanish.
verses.18: The caravans that travel beside them turn away.
verses.20: They were distressed because they were confident.
verses.22: Did I ever say, ‘Give to me’?
verses.23: or, ‘Deliver me from the adversary’s hand’?
verses.24: “Teach me, and I will hold my peace.
verses.25: How forcible are words of uprightness!
verses.26: Do you intend to reprove words,
verses.27: Yes, you would even cast lots for the fatherless,
verses.28: Now therefore be pleased to look at me,
verses.30: Is there injustice on my tongue?
